   124→
   125→== Overview
   126→
   127→The
   128→{jjdgr_gallops}
   129→is the persistent data store for Job Jockey.
   130→It tracks
   131→{jjdhr_heat_s}
   132→(bounded initiatives) and their constituent
   133→{jjdpr_pace_s}
   134→(discrete actions).
   135→Each
   136→{jjdpr_pace}
   137→maintains a history of
   138→{jjdkr_tack_s}
   139→(plan refinements).
   140→
   141→== Design Principles
   142→
   143→=== Exclusive JSON Ownership
   144→
   145→All access to the
   146→{jjdgr_gallops}
   147→goes through {jjdx_cli}, the Job Jockey subcommand of the Voce Viva executor.
   148→No jq or direct JSON manipulation ever touches this data.
   149→This is an absolute architectural constraint, not a preference.
   150→
   151→The rationale: writing reliable bash with jq pipelines requires fighting LLM training data.
   152→The internet is full of bad bash.
   153→Rust replaces fragile jq invocations with compiler-enforced correctness.
   154→The compiler is tireless; humans are not.
   155→
   156→=== Git as Journal
   157→
   158→The
   159→{jjdgr_gallops}
   160→remains JSON specifically so git can serve as the journalling layer.
   161→Every mutation creates a diffable commit.
   162→Git owns the complete history; we need not implement our own audit trail.
   163→
   164→The
   165→{jjdkr_tack}
   166→history within each
   167→{jjdpr_pace}
   168→exists for convenient postmortem analysis.
   169→Git has the full history, but
   170→{jjdkr_tack_s}
   171→let us see plan evolution without spelunking commits.
   172→
   173→=== Diff-Friendly Mutations
   174→
   175→New
   176→{jjdkr_tack_s}
   177→prepend to position 0 rather than append.
   178→This produces cleaner git diffs: prepending adds a line with trailing comma, while appending would modify the previous last line to add a comma.
   179→Small discipline, large cumulative benefit.
   180→
   181→The
   182→{jjdhm_order}
   183→array contains full
   184→{jjdt_coronet_s}.
   185→Each
   186→{jjdt_coronet}
   187→embeds its parent
   188→{jjdt_firemark}
   189→(redundant within a single
   190→{jjdhr_heat}).
   191→This redundancy is cheap and enables grep, copy-paste, and self-documenting JSON.
   192→
   193→=== Crash-Safe Architecture
   194→
   195→Bash handles locking via `git update-ref`.
   196→{jjdx_cli} owns all
   197→{jjdgr_gallops}
   198→file access - reading, transforming, and writing.
   199→{jjdx_cli} implements atomic writes internally (write to temp file, then rename).
   200→If {jjdx_cli} crashes mid-transform, no partial writes occur.
   201→
   202→The pattern:
   203→[source,bash]
   204→----
   205→lock_acquire
   206→vvx jjx gallops nominate --file gallops.json "my-heat" "260111" || { lock_release; die; }
   207→lock_release
   208→----
   209→
   210→This keeps bash simple (just locking and invocation) while {jjdx_cli} owns all JSON file operations.
   211→
   212→=== Deterministic Serialization
   213→
   214→Implementations MUST serialize JSON object keys in deterministic order to minimize diff churn.
   215→For `heats` and `paces` objects, use sorted key order (e.g., Rust `BTreeMap` instead of `HashMap`).
   216→This ensures that reordering paces via
   217→{jjdo_rail}
   218→produces minimal diffs — only the `order` array changes, not the entire `paces` object.
   219→
   220→=== Identity Encoding
   221→
   222→{jjdt_firemark}
   223→(`₣` prefix) and
   224→{jjdt_coronet}
   225→(`₢` prefix) are the canonical identities used in JSON keys, CLI arguments, and display.
   226→The base64 encoding provides compact, URL-safe, shell-safe strings.
   227→Internally, Rust may decode to integers for arithmetic, but the string form is authoritative.
   228→
   229→=== Horse Racing Vocabulary
   230→
   231→Job Jockey uses horse racing metaphors throughout.
   232→This is intentional: vivid domain vocabulary helps humans hold complex systems in working memory.
   233→
   234→* {jjdhr_heat} - a bounded initiative (like a race)
   235→* {jjdpr_pace} - a discrete action (the rhythm of the race)
   236→* {jjdkr_tack} - plan refinement (equipment; also "tacking" = adjusting course)
   237→* {jjdt_firemark} - heat identity, `₣`-prefixed (hot-iron brand marking ownership)
   238→* {jjdt_coronet} - pace identity, `₢`-prefixed (marking at the hoof line)
   239→* {jjdt_silks} - display name (jockey's colors)
   240→* {jjdgr_gallops} - the training grounds (where real work happens before race day)
   241→
   242→== Types
   243→
   244→[[jjdt_firemark]]
   245→// ⟦axl_voices axt_string⟧
   246→{jjdt_firemark}::
   247→The identity of a
   248→{jjdhr_heat}.
   249→Format: `₣` + 2 base64 characters (e.g., `₣AB`).
   250→The 2 base64 characters encode an integer 0-4095 (2^12).
   251→Used as JSON keys and in CLI arguments.
   252→Input may omit the `₣` prefix; output always includes it.
   253→
   254→[[jjdt_coronet]]
   255→// ⟦axl_voices axt_string⟧
   256→{jjdt_coronet}::
   257→The identity of a
   258→{jjdpr_pace}.
   259→Format: `₢` + 5 base64 characters (e.g., `₢ABCDE`).
   260→Globally unique: the first 2 base64 characters are the parent
   261→{jjdhr_heat_p}
   262→encoded identity; the remaining 3 encode the pace index (0-262143, 2^18).
   263→Used as JSON keys and in CLI arguments.
   264→Input may omit the `₢` prefix; output always includes it.
   265→+
   266→*Reassignment:* When a
   267→{jjdpr_pace}
   268→is moved between
   269→{jjdhr_heat_s}
   270→via
   271→{jjdo_draft},
   272→the old
   273→{jjdt_coronet}
   274→becomes invalid and a new one is assigned using the destination
   275→{jjdhr_heat_p}
   276→{jjdhm_seed}.
   277→The new
   278→{jjdt_coronet}
   279→embeds the destination
   280→{jjdt_firemark}.
   281→{jjdkr_tack}
   282→history is preserved, with a note recording the original
   283→{jjdt_coronet}.
   284→
   285→[[jjdt_silks]]
   286→// ⟦axl_voices axt_string⟧
   287→{jjdt_silks}::
   288→A kebab-case display name.
   289→Used for human-readable identification of
   290→{jjdhr_heat_s}
   291→and
   292→{jjdpr_pace_s}.
   293→Pattern: `[a-z0-9]+(-[a-z0-9]+)*`
   294→
   295→== Records
   296→
   297→=== {jjdgr_gallops}
   298→
   299→[[jjdgr_gallops]]
   300→// ⟦axl_voices axr_record_json⟧
   301→{jjdgr_gallops}::
   302→The root JSON structure containing all Job Jockey data.
   303→Singleton record persisted at `.claude/jjm/jjg_gallops.json`.
   304→
   305→==== Members
   306→
   307→* [[jjdgm_seed]]
   308→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   309→{jjdgm_seed}
   310→is the next
   311→{jjdt_firemark}
   312→to allocate, stored as 2 base64 characters.
   313→Incremented by
   314→{jjdo_nominate}.
   315→
   316→=== {jjdhr_heat}
   317→
   318→[[jjdhr_heat]]
   319→// ⟦axl_voices axr_record_json⟧
   320→{jjdhr_heat}::
   321→A bounded initiative with coherent goals.
   322→Contains zero or more
   323→{jjdpr_pace_s}
   324→representing discrete actions.
   325→Keyed in JSON by
   326→{jjdt_firemark}.
   327→
   328→==== Members
   329→
   330→* [[jjdhm_firemark]]
   331→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   332→{jjdhm_firemark}
   333→is the
   334→{jjdhr_heat_p}
   335→primary key.
   336→Type:
   337→{jjdt_firemark}.
   338→Implicit from JSON key; not stored as separate field.
   339→
   340→* [[jjdhm_silks]]
   341→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   342→{jjdhm_silks}
   343→is the
   344→{jjdhr_heat_p}
   345→display name.
   346→Type:
   347→{jjdt_silks}.
   348→
   349→* [[jjdhm_created]]
   350→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   351→{jjdhm_created}
   352→records when the
   353→{jjdhr_heat}
   354→was nominated.
   355→Format: YYMMDD.
   356→
   357→* [[jjdhm_status]]
   358→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   359→{jjdhm_status}
   360→indicates the
   361→{jjdhr_heat_p}
   362→lifecycle state.
   363→Values:
   364→{jjdhe_current}
   365→or
   366→{jjdhe_retired}.
   367→
   368→* [[jjdhm_order]]
   369→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required axd_repeated⟧
   370→{jjdhm_order}
   371→lists
   372→{jjdt_coronet_s}
   373→in execution sequence.
   374→May be reordered via
   375→{jjdo_rail}.
   376→
   377→* [[jjdhm_seed]]
   378→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   379→{jjdhm_seed}
   380→is the next
   381→{jjdt_coronet}
   382→index to allocate, stored as 3 base64 characters.
   383→Incremented by
   384→{jjdo_slate}.
   385→
   386→* [[jjdhm_paddock]]
   387→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   388→{jjdhm_paddock}
   389→is the path to the paddock file containing
   390→{jjdhr_heat}
   391→context and background.
   392→Format: `.claude/jjm/jjp_XX.md` where XX is the base64-encoded
   393→{jjdt_firemark}
   394→(without `₣` prefix).
   395→Created by
   396→{jjdo_nominate}.
   397→
   398→==== Status Values
   399→
   400→[[jjdhe_current]]
   401→// ⟦axl_voices axt_enum_value⟧
   402→{jjdhe_current}::
   403→The
   404→{jjdhr_heat}
   405→is active and accepting work.
   406→
   407→[[jjdhe_retired]]
   408→// ⟦axl_voices axt_enum_value⟧
   409→{jjdhe_retired}::
   410→The
   411→{jjdhr_heat}
   412→is complete and archived.
   413→
   414→=== {jjdpr_pace}
   415→
   416→[[jjdpr_pace]]
   417→// ⟦axl_voices axr_record_json⟧
   418→{jjdpr_pace}::
   419→A discrete action within a
   420→{jjdhr_heat}.
   421→Contains one or more
   422→{jjdkr_tack_s}
   423→representing state snapshots and plan refinements.
   424→Current state is derived from `tacks[0].state`.
   425→Current silks is derived from `tacks[0].silks`.
   426→Keyed in JSON by
   427→{jjdt_coronet}.
   428→
   429→==== Members
   430→
   431→* [[jjdpm_coronet]]
   432→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   433→{jjdpm_coronet}
   434→is the
   435→{jjdpr_pace_p}
   436→globally unique identity.
   437→Type:
   438→{jjdt_coronet}.
   439→Implicit from JSON key; not stored as separate field.
   440→
   441→=== {jjdkr_tack}
   442→
   443→[[jjdkr_tack]]
   444→// ⟦axl_voices axr_record_json⟧
   445→{jjdkr_tack}::
   446→A snapshot of
   447→{jjdpr_pace}
   448→state and plan at a point in time.
   449→{jjdkr_tack_s}
   450→form an append-only history capturing both plan refinements and state transitions.
   451→Position 0 is the most recent; older
   452→{jjdkr_tack_s}
   453→follow.
   454→New
   455→{jjdkr_tack_s}
   456→are prepended for cleaner git diffs.
   457→The current
   458→{jjdpr_pace}
   459→state is always
   460→`tacks[0].state`.
   461→
   462→==== Members
   463→
   464→* [[jjdkm_refined]]
   465→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   466→{jjdkm_refined}
   467→records when this
   468→{jjdkr_tack}
   469→was added.
   470→Format: YYMMDD-HHMM.
   471→
   472→* [[jjdkm_state]]
   473→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   474→{jjdkm_state}
   475→indicates the
   476→{jjdpr_pace_p}
   477→progress at this snapshot.
   478→Values:
   479→{jjdpe_rough},
   480→{jjdpe_bridled},
   481→{jjdpe_complete},
   482→or
   483→{jjdpe_abandoned}.
   484→
   485→* [[jjdkm_text]]
   486→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   487→{jjdkm_text}
   488→contains the plan specification.
   489→Non-empty string describing what the
   490→{jjdpr_pace}
   491→should accomplish.
   492→
   493→* [[jjdkm_silks]]
   494→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   495→{jjdkm_silks}
   496→is the
   497→{jjdpr_pace_p}
   498→display name at this point in time.
   499→Type:
   500→{jjdt_silks}.
   501→Enables rename history; current pace silks is derived from `tacks[0].silks`.
   502→
   503→* [[jjdkm_commit]]
   504→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   505→{jjdkm_commit}
   506→is the commit SHA at
   507→{jjdkr_tack}
   508→creation.
   509→Format: 7-character hex string.
   510→Captures repo state when plan was written.
   511→Value `0000000` indicates unknown (migrated data).
   512→
   513→* [[jjdkm_direction]]
   514→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_conditional⟧
   515→{jjdkm_direction}
   516→contains execution guidance for autonomous operation.
   517→Required when
   518→{jjdkm_state}
   519→is
   520→{jjdpe_bridled};
   521→forbidden otherwise.
   522→Describes which agent to dispatch, parallelism strategy, or other execution parameters.
   523→
   524→==== State Values
   525→
   526→[[jjdpe_rough]]
   527→// ⟦axl_voices axt_enum_value⟧
   528→{jjdpe_rough}::
   529→The
   530→{jjdpr_pace}
   531→needs clarification before work can proceed.
   532→When context is assembled for a
   533→{jjdpe_rough}
   534→pace, the model is guided to refine the specification rather than begin execution.
   535→
   536→[[jjdpe_bridled]]
   537→// ⟦axl_voices axt_enum_value⟧
   538→{jjdpe_bridled}::
   539→The
   540→{jjdpr_pace}
   541→is ready for autonomous execution.
   542→The
   543→{jjdkm_direction}
   544→field contains explicit guidance for the executing agent.
   545→When context is assembled for a
   546→{jjdpe_bridled}
   547→pace, the model proceeds without human confirmation.
   548→
   549→[[jjdpe_complete]]
   550→// ⟦axl_voices axt_enum_value⟧
   551→{jjdpe_complete}::
   552→The
   553→{jjdpr_pace}
   554→has been successfully finished.
   555→
   556→[[jjdpe_abandoned]]
   557→// ⟦axl_voices axt_enum_value⟧
   558→{jjdpe_abandoned}::
   559→The
   560→{jjdpr_pace}
   561→was stopped without completion.
   562→
   563→== Serialization
   564→
   565→[[jjdz_charset]]
   566→{jjdz_charset}::
   567→The 64-character alphabet for identity encoding.
   568→Uses URL-safe base64 (RFC 4648 §5):
   569→`ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789-_`
   570→Position in charset determines numeric value (A=0, B=1, ..., `-`=62, `_`=63).
   571→This alphabet is safe for JSON keys, URLs, shell, git refs, and filenames.
   572→Matches Rust `base64::engine::general_purpose::URL_SAFE_NO_PAD`.
   573→
   574→[[jjdz_encoding]]
   575→{jjdz_encoding}::
   576→The algorithm for encoding
   577→{jjdt_firemark}
   578→and
   579→{jjdt_coronet}
   580→values.
   581→+
   582→*{jjdt_firemark} (Heat identity):*
   583→+
   584→. Format: `₣` + 2 base64 characters
   585→. Given integer H (0-4095): encode as `₣` + `charset[H/64]` + `charset[H%64]`
   586→. To decode `₣XY`: H = pos(X) * 64 + pos(Y)
   587→+
   588→*{jjdt_coronet} (Pace identity):*
   589→+
   590→. Format: `₢` + 5 base64 characters
   591→. First 2 characters encode the parent Heat (same as {jjdt_firemark} without prefix)
   592→. Last 3 characters encode the pace index P (0-262143)
   593→. Given Heat H and pace index P: encode as `₢` + `charset[H/64]` + `charset[H%64]` + `charset[P/4096]` + `charset[(P/64)%64]` + `charset[P%64]`
   594→. To decode `₢XYZAB`: H from XY, P = pos(Z) * 4096 + pos(A) * 64 + pos(B)
   595→+
   596→*Input flexibility:*
   597→+
   598→CLI commands accept identities with or without prefix.
   599→Length determines type: 2 base64 chars = {jjdt_firemark}, 5 base64 chars = {jjdt_coronet}.
   600→Output always includes the prefix (`₣` or `₢`).
   601→
   602→=== Legacy Format Acceptance
   603→
   604→During migration period, implementations MUST accept legacy format on read:
   605→
   606→* {jjdpr_pace} with `silks` field → copy to all {jjdkr_tack_s} missing {jjdkm_silks}, discard from {jjdpr_pace}
   607→* {jjdkr_tack} without {jjdkm_silks} → inherit from {jjdpr_pace} (error if {jjdpr_pace} also missing silks)
   608→* {jjdkr_tack} without {jjdkm_commit} → use `0000000`
   609→
   610→Implementations MUST write only canonical format:
   611→
   612→* {jjdpr_pace} without `silks` field
   613→* {jjdkr_tack} with {jjdkm_silks} (required)
   614→* {jjdkr_tack} with {jjdkm_commit} (required, `0000000` if unknown)
   615→

   801→== CLI
   802→
   803→[[jjdx_vvx]]
   804→// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_program⟧
   805→{jjdx_vvx}::
   806→The Voce Viva executor.
   807→Local reference to the VVK-defined CLI program that hosts Job Jockey.
   808→
   809→[[jjdx_cli]]
   810→// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_command_group⟧
   811→{jjdx_cli}::
   812→The Job Jockey command group within
   813→{jjdx_vvx}.
   814→Invoked as `vvx jjx`.
   815→All operations are subcommands of
   816→{jjdx_cli}.
   817→Inherits Unix CLI semantics: stdout for results, stderr for errors, exit 0 for success, non-zero for failure.
   818→
   819→== Commit Message Architecture
   820→
   821→All JJ commits include an action code that serves multiple purposes:
   822→- **Parsing steeplechase history**: Distinguishes between different operation types
   823→- **Semantic clarity**: Indicates the nature and context of each commit
   824→- **Consistency**: Applies uniformly across pace-level and heat-level operations
   825→
   826→== Operations
   827→
   828→Operations are implemented as {jjdx_cli} subcommands.
   829→{jjdx_cli} owns all
   830→{jjdgr_gallops}
   831→file access (read, transform, atomic write).
   832→Bash handles locking via `git update-ref` and invokes {jjdx_cli} with
   833→{jjda_file}.
   834→
   835→=== Write Operations
   836→
   837→[[jjdo_nominate]]
   838→// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
   839→==== {jjdo_nominate}
   840→
   841→Create a new
   842→{jjdhr_heat}
   843→with empty
   844→{jjdpr_pace}
   845→structure.
   846→
   847→{jjds_arguments}
   848→
   849→// ⟦axd_optional axd_defaulted⟧
   850→* {jjda_file}
   851→
   852→// ⟦axd_required⟧
   853→* {jjda_silks}
   854→
   855→// ⟦axd_required⟧
   856→* {jjda_created}
   857→
   858→{jjds_stdout} New
   859→{jjdt_firemark}
   860→
   861→{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
   862→
   863→{jjds_behavior}
   864→
   865→. If
   866→{jjdgr_gallops}
   867→file does not exist:
   868→.. Create parent directory `.claude/jjm/` if needed
   869→.. Initialize new
   870→{jjdgr_gallops}
   871→with
   872→{jjdgm_seed}
   873→`AA` and empty `heats`
   874→. Read
   875→{jjdgr_gallops}
   876→from file (or use newly created)
   877→. Read current
   878→{jjdgm_seed}
   879→as
   880→{jjdt_firemark}
   881→. Compute paddock path: `.claude/jjm/jjp_XX.md` where XX is the 2 base64 chars
   882→. Create paddock file with template:
   883→+
   884→----
   885→# Paddock: {silks}
   886→
   887→## Context
   888→
   889→(Describe the initiative's background and goals)
   890→
   891→## References
   892→
   893→(List relevant files, docs, or prior work)
   894→----
   895→. Create new
   896→{jjdhr_heat}
   897→with:
   898→.. Key: `₣` + encoded
   899→{jjdt_firemark}
   900→.. {jjdhm_silks}:
   901→from argument
   902→.. {jjdhm_created}:
   903→from argument
   904→.. {jjdhm_status}:
   905→{jjdhe_current}
   906→.. {jjdhm_order}:
   907→empty array
   908→.. {jjdhm_seed}:
   909→`AAA` (index 0)
   910→.. {jjdhm_paddock}:
   911→computed paddock path
   912→.. `paces`: empty object
   913→. Increment
   914→{jjdgm_seed}
   915→(base64 arithmetic with carry)
   916→. Write transformed
   917→{jjdgr_gallops}
   918→to file (atomic)
   919→. Output new
   920→{jjdt_firemark}
   921→to stdout
   922→
   923→[[jjdo_slate]]
   924→// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
   925→==== {jjdo_slate}
   926→
   927→Add a new
   928→{jjdpr_pace}
   929→to a
   930→{jjdhr_heat}.
   931→The initial
   932→{jjdkr_tack}
   933→is created with state
   934→{jjdpe_rough}.
   935→
   936→By default, the new
   937→{jjdpr_pace}
   938→is appended to the end of
   939→{jjdhm_order}.
   940→Use positioning flags to insert at a specific location.
   941→
   942→{jjds_arguments}
   943→
   944→// ⟦axd_optional axd_defaulted⟧
   945→* {jjda_file}
   946→
   947→// ⟦axd_required⟧
   948→* {jjdt_firemark}
   949→(positional) — target
   950→{jjdhr_heat}
   951→
   952→// ⟦axd_required⟧
   953→* {jjda_silks}
   954→
   955→// ⟦axd_required⟧
   956→* stdin —
   957→{jjdkm_text}
   958→content for the initial
   959→{jjdkr_tack}
   960→
   961→// ⟦axd_optional⟧
   962→* {jjda_before}
   963→— insert before specified
   964→{jjdt_coronet}
   965→
   966→// ⟦axd_optional⟧
   967→* {jjda_after}
   968→— insert after specified
   969→{jjdt_coronet}
   970→
   971→// ⟦axd_optional⟧
   972→* {jjda_first}
   973→— insert at beginning of
   974→{jjdhm_order}
   975→
   976→*Positioning:* Only one of
   977→{jjda_before},
   978→{jjda_after},
   979→or
   980→{jjda_first}
   981→may be specified.
   982→If none provided, append to end (default).
   983→Note:
   984→{jjdo_rail}
   985→reorders existing
   986→{jjdpr_pace_s};
   987→{jjdo_slate}
   988→positioning inserts a new
   989→{jjdpr_pace}
   990→at a specific location.
   991→
   992→{jjds_stdout} New
   993→{jjdt_coronet}
   994→
   995→{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
   996→
   997→{jjds_behavior}
   998→
   999→. Read
  1000→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1001→from file
  1002→. Verify
  1003→{jjdhr_heat}
  1004→exists with given
  1005→{jjdt_firemark}
  1006→. If
  1007→{jjda_before}
  1008→or
  1009→{jjda_after}
  1010→provided, verify target
  1011→{jjdt_coronet}
  1012→exists in
  1013→{jjdhr_heat}
  1014→. Read
  1015→{jjdhm_seed}
  1016→from target
  1017→{jjdhr_heat}
  1018→. Construct
  1019→{jjdt_coronet}:
  1020→`₢` + encoded
  1021→{jjdt_firemark}
  1022→(2 chars) +
  1023→{jjdhm_seed}
  1024→(3 chars)
  1025→. Create new
  1026→{jjdpr_pace}
  1027→with:
  1028→.. Key: constructed
  1029→{jjdt_coronet}
  1030→.. `tacks`: array with single
  1031→{jjdkr_tack}:
  1032→... {jjdkm_refined}:
  1033→current timestamp (YYMMDD-HHMM)
  1034→... {jjdkm_state}:
  1035→{jjdpe_rough}
  1036→... {jjdkm_text}:
  1037→from stdin
  1038→... {jjdkm_silks}:
  1039→from argument
  1040→... {jjdkm_commit}:
  1041→capture {jjdkm_commit}
  1042→. Insert
  1043→{jjdt_coronet}
  1044→into
  1045→{jjdhm_order}
  1046→at determined position:
  1047→.. If
  1048→{jjda_first}:
  1049→prepend to beginning
  1050→.. If
  1051→{jjda_before}:
  1052→insert immediately before target
  1053→{jjdt_coronet}
  1054→.. If
  1055→{jjda_after}:
  1056→insert immediately after target
  1057→{jjdt_coronet}
  1058→.. Otherwise: append to end
  1059→. Increment
  1060→{jjdhm_seed}
  1061→(base64 arithmetic with carry)
  1062→. Write transformed
  1063→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1064→to file (atomic)
  1065→. Output new
  1066→{jjdt_coronet}
  1067→to stdout
  1068→
  1069→[[jjdo_rail]]
  1070→// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
  1071→==== {jjdo_rail}
  1072→
  1073→Reorder
  1074→{jjdpr_pace_s}
  1075→within a
  1076→{jjdhr_heat}
  1077→by updating
  1078→{jjdhm_order}.
  1079→Supports two modes: *order mode* replaces the entire sequence; *move mode* relocates a single
  1080→{jjdpr_pace}.
  1081→
  1082→*Order mode* (full replacement):
  1083→----
  1084→vvx jjx_rail <FIREMARK> <CORONET>...
  1085→----
  1086→
  1087→*Move mode* (single relocation):
  1088→----
  1089→vvx jjx_rail <FIREMARK> --move <CORONET> [--before <TARGET> | --after <TARGET> | --first | --last]
  1090→----
  1091→
  1092→{jjds_arguments}
  1093→
  1094→// ⟦axd_optional axd_defaulted⟧
  1095→* {jjda_file}
  1096→
  1097→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  1098→* {jjdt_firemark}
  1099→(positional) — target
  1100→{jjdhr_heat}
  1101→
  1102→*Order mode arguments:*
  1103→
  1104→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  1105→* order — array of
  1106→{jjdt_coronet_s}
  1107→in desired sequence (space-separated positional arguments)
  1108→
  1109→*Move mode arguments:*
  1110→
  1111→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  1112→* {jjda_move}
  1113→— {jjdt_coronet}
  1114→to relocate
  1115→
  1116→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  1117→* Exactly one positioning flag:
  1118→{jjda_before},
  1119→{jjda_after},
  1120→{jjda_first},
  1121→or
  1122→{jjda_last}
  1123→
  1124→*Mode detection:* If
  1125→{jjda_move}
  1126→is present, move mode; otherwise order mode.
  1127→
  1128→{jjds_stdout} Updated
  1129→{jjdhm_order}
  1130→array, one
  1131→{jjdt_coronet}
  1132→per line, in new sequence.
  1133→
  1134→{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
  1135→
  1136→{jjds_behavior}
  1137→
  1138→*Common steps:*
  1139→
  1140→. Read
  1141→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1142→from file
  1143→. Verify
  1144→{jjdhr_heat}
  1145→exists with given
  1146→{jjdt_firemark}
  1147→
  1148→*Order mode:*
  1149→
  1150→. Parse positional arguments into array of
  1151→{jjdt_coronet_s}
  1152→. Validate order array:
  1153→.. Count must match current
  1154→{jjdhm_order}
  1155→length
  1156→.. No duplicate
  1157→{jjdt_coronet_s}
  1158→.. All
  1159→{jjdt_coronet_s}
  1160→must exist in
  1161→{jjdhr_heat_p}
  1162→`paces` object
  1163→.. All
  1164→{jjdt_coronet_s}
  1165→must embed correct parent
  1166→{jjdt_firemark}
  1167→. Replace
  1168→{jjdhm_order}
  1169→with new order array
  1170→. Write transformed
  1171→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1172→to file (atomic)
  1173→. Output new
  1174→{jjdhm_order},
  1175→one
  1176→{jjdt_coronet}
  1177→per line
  1178→
  1179→*Move mode:*
  1180→
  1181→. Validate
  1182→{jjda_move}
  1183→{jjdt_coronet}
  1184→exists in
  1185→{jjdhr_heat}
  1186→. Validate exactly one positioning flag provided
  1187→. If
  1188→{jjda_before}
  1189→or
  1190→{jjda_after}:
  1191→validate target
  1192→{jjdt_coronet}
  1193→exists and differs from
  1194→{jjda_move}
  1195→{jjdt_coronet}
  1196→. Remove
  1197→{jjda_move}
  1198→{jjdt_coronet}
  1199→from current position in
  1200→{jjdhm_order}
  1201→. Insert at new position:
  1202→.. If
  1203→{jjda_first}:
  1204→prepend to beginning
  1205→.. If
  1206→{jjda_last}:
  1207→append to end
  1208→.. If
  1209→{jjda_before}:
  1210→insert immediately before target
  1211→{jjdt_coronet}
  1212→.. If
  1213→{jjda_after}:
  1214→insert immediately after target
  1215→{jjdt_coronet}
  1216→. Write transformed
  1217→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1218→to file (atomic)
  1219→. Output new
  1220→{jjdhm_order},
  1221→one
  1222→{jjdt_coronet}
  1223→per line

  1251→// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
  1252→==== {jjdo_draft}
  1253→
  1254→Move a
  1255→{jjdpr_pace}
  1256→from one
  1257→{jjdhr_heat}
  1258→to another.
  1259→This is a primitive operation: mechanical transfer with no ceremony.
  1260→The
  1261→{jjdpr_pace}
  1262→receives a new
  1263→{jjdt_coronet}
  1264→in the destination
  1265→{jjdhr_heat};
  1266→all
  1267→{jjdkr_tack}
  1268→history is preserved.
  1269→
  1270→*Important:* "Drafted" is not a
  1271→{jjdpr_pace}
  1272→state.
  1273→{jjdpr_pace}
  1274→states remain:
  1275→{jjdpe_rough},
  1276→{jjdpe_bridled},
  1277→{jjdpe_complete},
  1278→{jjdpe_abandoned}.
  1279→Draft is an operation that moves
  1280→{jjdpr_pace_s}
  1281→between
  1282→{jjdhr_heat_s};
  1283→state is preserved through the move.
  1284→
  1285→{jjds_arguments}
  1286→
  1287→// ⟦axd_optional axd_defaulted⟧
  1288→* {jjda_file}
  1289→
  1290→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  1291→* {jjdt_coronet}
  1292→(positional) —
  1293→{jjdpr_pace}
  1294→to move
  1295→
  1296→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  1297→* {jjda_to}
  1298→— destination
  1299→{jjdt_firemark}
  1300→
  1301→// ⟦axd_optional⟧
  1302→* {jjda_before}
  1303→— insert before specified
  1304→{jjdt_coronet}
  1305→in destination
  1306→
  1307→// ⟦axd_optional⟧
  1308→* {jjda_after}
  1309→— insert after specified
  1310→{jjdt_coronet}
  1311→in destination
  1312→
  1313→// ⟦axd_optional⟧
  1314→* {jjda_first}
  1315→— insert at beginning of destination
  1316→{jjdhm_order}
  1317→
  1318→*Positioning:* Only one of
  1319→{jjda_before},
  1320→{jjda_after},
  1321→or
  1322→{jjda_first}
  1323→may be specified.
  1324→If none provided, append to end of destination
  1325→{jjdhm_order}
  1326→(default).
  1327→
  1328→{jjds_stdout} New
  1329→{jjdt_coronet}
  1330→in destination
  1331→{jjdhr_heat}
  1332→
  1333→{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
  1334→
  1335→{jjds_behavior}
  1336→
  1337→. Read
  1338→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1339→from file
  1340→. Extract source
  1341→{jjdt_firemark}
  1342→from
  1343→{jjdt_coronet}
  1344→(first 2 base64 chars after prefix)
  1345→. Verify source
  1346→{jjdhr_heat}
  1347→exists
  1348→. Verify
  1349→{jjdpr_pace}
  1350→exists in source
  1351→{jjdhr_heat}
  1352→. Verify destination
  1353→{jjdhr_heat}
  1354→exists with given
  1355→{jjdt_firemark}
  1356→. If
  1357→{jjda_before}
  1358→or
  1359→{jjda_after}
  1360→provided, verify target
  1361→{jjdt_coronet}
  1362→exists in destination
  1363→{jjdhr_heat}
  1364→. Read destination
  1365→{jjdhm_seed}
  1366→. Construct new
  1367→{jjdt_coronet}:
  1368→`₢` + destination
  1369→{jjdt_firemark}
  1370→(2 chars) + destination
  1371→{jjdhm_seed}
  1372→(3 chars)
  1373→. Copy
  1374→{jjdpr_pace}
  1375→data to destination with new
  1376→{jjdt_coronet}:
  1377→.. Prepend new
  1378→{jjdkr_tack}
  1379→recording the draft:
  1380→... {jjdkm_refined}:
  1381→current timestamp (YYMMDD-HHMM)
  1382→... {jjdkm_state}:
  1383→inherited from previous `tacks[0].state`
  1384→... {jjdkm_text}:
  1385→`Drafted from {old_coronet} in {source_firemark}.\n\n` + previous `tacks[0].text`
  1386→... {jjdkm_silks}:
  1387→inherited from previous `tacks[0].silks`
  1388→... {jjdkm_commit}:
  1389→capture {jjdkm_commit}
  1390→.. Preserve remaining
  1391→{jjdkr_tack}
  1392→history
  1393→. Insert new
  1394→{jjdt_coronet}
  1395→into destination
  1396→{jjdhm_order}
  1397→at determined position
  1398→. Increment destination
  1399→{jjdhm_seed}
  1400→. Remove
  1401→{jjdpr_pace}
  1402→from source
  1403→{jjdhr_heat}:
  1404→.. Remove from source `paces` object
  1405→.. Remove from source
  1406→{jjdhm_order}
  1407→. Write transformed
  1408→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1409→to file (atomic)
  1410→. Output new
  1411→{jjdt_coronet}
  1412→to stdout
  1413→
  1414→*Validation errors:*
  1415→
  1416→[cols="2,3"]
  1417→|===
  1418→| Condition | Error
  1419→
  1420→| Source and destination same
  1421→| "Cannot draft pace to same heat"
  1422→
  1423→| Source {jjdpr_pace} not found
  1424→| "Pace {coronet} not found in heat {firemark}"
  1425→
  1426→| Destination {jjdhr_heat} not found
  1427→| "Heat {firemark} not found"
  1428→
  1429→| Target {jjdt_coronet} not found in destination
  1430→| "Target pace {coronet} not found in heat {firemark}"
  1431→|===

  1551→[[jjdo_retire]]
  1552→// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
  1553→==== {jjdo_retire}
  1554→
  1555→Archive a
  1556→{jjdhr_heat}
  1557→by writing a trophy file, removing the heat from
  1558→{jjdgr_gallops},
  1559→deleting the paddock file, and committing.
  1560→This is a destructive operation that permanently removes the heat from active tracking.
  1561→
  1562→{jjds_arguments}
  1563→
  1564→// ⟦axd_optional axd_defaulted⟧
  1565→* {jjda_file}
  1566→
  1567→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  1568→* {jjdt_firemark}
  1569→(positional) — target
  1570→{jjdhr_heat}
  1571→
  1572→// ⟦axd_optional⟧
  1573→* `--execute` — perform the retire operation.
  1574→Without this flag, outputs trophy markdown to stdout as a dry-run preview.
  1575→
  1576→{jjds_stdout}
  1577→Without `--execute`: trophy markdown content (dry-run preview).
  1578→With `--execute`: path to the written trophy file.
  1579→
  1580→{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
  1581→
  1582→{jjds_behavior}
  1583→
  1584→Without `--execute` (dry-run):
  1585→
  1586→. Read
  1587→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1588→from file
  1589→. Verify
  1590→{jjdhr_heat}
  1591→exists with given
  1592→{jjdt_firemark}
  1593→. Read paddock file contents from
  1594→{jjdhm_paddock}
  1595→. Call
  1596→{jjdo_rein}
  1597→internally to get steeplechase entries
  1598→. Build trophy markdown (see format below)
  1599→. Output trophy markdown to stdout
  1600→
  1601→With `--execute`:
  1602→
  1603→. Acquire lock (`refs/vvg/locks/vvx`)
  1604→. Read
  1605→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1606→from file
  1607→. Verify
  1608→{jjdhr_heat}
  1609→exists with given
  1610→{jjdt_firemark}
  1611→. Extract
  1612→{jjdhr_heat}
  1613→metadata:
  1614→{jjdhm_silks},
  1615→{jjdhm_created}
  1616→. Read paddock file contents from
  1617→{jjdhm_paddock}
  1618→. Call
  1619→{jjdo_rein}
  1620→internally to get steeplechase entries
  1621→. Build trophy markdown:
  1622→+
  1623→----
  1624→# Heat Trophy: {silks}
  1625→
  1626→**Firemark:** {firemark}
  1627→**Created:** {created}
  1628→**Retired:** {today}
  1629→**Status:** retired
  1630→
  1631→## Paddock
  1632→
  1633→{full paddock content}
  1634→
  1635→## Paces
  1636→
  1637→{for each pace in order:}
  1638→
  1639→### {pace_silks} ({coronet}) [{final_state}]
  1640→
  1641→{for each tack, newest first:}
  1642→**[{ts}] {state}**
  1643→
  1644→{tack text}
  1645→
  1646→{if tack has direction:}
  1647→*Direction:* {direction}
  1648→
  1649→## Steeplechase
  1650→
  1651→{for each steeplechase entry from rein, newest first:}
  1652→
  1653→### {date} - {pace_silks or "Heat"} - {marker or "commit"}
  1654→
  1655→{subject/description}
  1656→
  1657→----
  1658→. Compute trophy filename: `.claude/jjm/retired/jjh_b{created}-r{today}-{silks}.md`
  1659→where `{today}` is current date in YYMMDD format
  1660→. Create `.claude/jjm/retired/` directory if it does not exist
  1661→. Write trophy markdown to trophy file
  1662→. Remove
  1663→{jjdhr_heat}
  1664→entry from
  1665→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1666→`heats` object
  1667→. Write transformed
  1668→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1669→to file (atomic)
  1670→. Delete paddock file at
  1671→{jjdhm_paddock}
  1672→. Stage exactly three paths: trophy file, gallops.json, paddock file (as deletion)
  1673→. Run size guard with 200KB limit (trophy files can be large for big heats)
  1674→. Commit with message per steeplechase conventions (format TBD by unify-commit-format)
  1675→. Release lock
  1676→. Output trophy file path to stdout
  1677→
  1678→=== Read Operations
  1679→
  1680→[[jjdo_validate]]
  1681→// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
  1682→==== {jjdo_validate}
  1683→
  1684→Check that
  1685→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1686→JSON conforms to schema.
  1687→
  1688→{jjds_arguments}
  1689→
  1690→// ⟦axd_optional axd_defaulted⟧
  1691→* {jjda_file}
  1692→
  1693→{jjds_stdout} None
  1694→
  1695→{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
  1696→
  1697→*Validation Rules:*
  1698→
  1699→. Root must be JSON object with
  1700→{jjdgm_seed}
  1701→(2 URL-safe base64 characters matching `[A-Za-z0-9_-]{2}`)
  1702→. Root must have `heats` object
  1703→. Each
  1704→{jjdhr_heat}
  1705→key must match `₣[A-Za-z0-9_-]{2}`
  1706→. Each
  1707→{jjdhr_heat}
  1708→must have:
  1709→{jjdhm_silks}
  1710→(non-empty kebab-case),
  1711→{jjdhm_created}
  1712→(YYMMDD),
  1713→{jjdhm_status}
  1714→({jjdhe_current}
  1715→or
  1716→{jjdhe_retired}),
  1717→{jjdhm_order}
  1718→(array),
  1719→{jjdhm_seed}
  1720→(3 URL-safe base64 characters matching `[A-Za-z0-9_-]{3}`),
  1721→`paces` (object)
  1722→. {jjdhm_order}
  1723→array and `paces` object must have identical key sets
  1724→. Each
  1725→{jjdpr_pace}
  1726→key must match `₢[A-Za-z0-9_-]{5}` and embed parent
  1727→{jjdhr_heat}
  1728→identity (first 2 base64 chars after prefix)
  1729→. Each
  1730→{jjdpr_pace}
  1731→must have:
  1732→`tacks` (non-empty array)
  1733→. Each
  1734→{jjdkr_tack}
  1735→must have:
  1736→{jjdkm_refined}
  1737→(YYMMDD-HHMM),
  1738→{jjdkm_state}
  1739→({jjdpe_rough},
  1740→{jjdpe_bridled},
  1741→{jjdpe_complete},
  1742→or
  1743→{jjdpe_abandoned}),
  1744→{jjdkm_text}
  1745→(non-empty string),
  1746→{jjdkm_silks}
  1747→(non-empty kebab-case),
  1748→{jjdkm_commit}
  1749→(valid {jjdkm_commit} format)
  1750→. If
  1751→{jjdkm_state}
  1752→is
  1753→{jjdpe_bridled},
  1754→{jjdkm_direction}
  1755→must be present (non-empty string); otherwise
  1756→{jjdkm_direction}
  1757→must be absent
  1758→

  1762→

  1764→{jjdhr_heat_s}

  1769→// ⟦axd_optional axd_defaulted⟧

  1771→
  1772→// ⟦axd_optional⟧

  1776→with this status; if absent, show all

  1780→`{jjdt_firemark}<TAB>{jjdt_silks}<TAB>{jjdhm_status}<TAB><pace_count>`

  1806→[[jjdo_saddle]]
  1807→// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
  1808→==== {jjdo_saddle}
  1809→
  1810→Return context needed to saddle up on a
  1811→{jjdhr_heat}:
  1812→the current
  1813→{jjdpr_pace}
  1814→(first in
  1815→{jjdhm_order}
  1816→whose state is
  1817→{jjdpe_rough}
  1818→or
  1819→{jjdpe_bridled})
  1820→and its
  1821→{jjdkr_tack}
  1822→details.
  1823→
  1824→{jjds_arguments}
  1825→
  1826→// ⟦axd_optional axd_defaulted⟧
  1827→* {jjda_file}
  1828→
  1829→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  1830→* {jjdt_firemark}
  1831→(positional) — target
  1832→{jjdhr_heat}
  1833→
  1834→{jjds_stdout} JSON object with structure:
  1835→[source,json]
  1836→----
  1837→{
  1838→  "heat_silks": "...",
  1839→  "paddock_file": ".claude/jjm/jjp_XX.md",
  1840→  "paddock_content": "...",
  1841→  "pace_coronet": "₢XXXXX",
  1842→  "pace_silks": "...",
  1843→  "pace_state": "rough|bridled",
  1844→  "tack_text": "...",
  1845→  "tack_direction": "..."
  1846→}
  1847→----
  1848→Fields `pace_coronet` through `tack_direction` are absent if no actionable pace exists.
  1849→Field `pace_silks` is derived from `tacks[0].silks`.
  1850→Field `tack_direction` is present only when `pace_state` is
  1851→{jjdpe_bridled}.
  1852→Field `paddock_content` contains the full text of the paddock file.
  1853→
  1854→{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
  1855→
  1856→{jjds_behavior}
  1857→
  1858→. Read
  1859→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1860→from file
  1861→. Verify
  1862→{jjdhr_heat}
  1863→exists with given
  1864→{jjdt_firemark}
  1865→. Output `heat_silks` from
  1866→{jjdhr_heat}
  1867→. Output `paddock_file` from
  1868→{jjdhm_paddock}
  1869→. Read paddock file contents, output as `paddock_content`
  1870→. Walk
  1871→{jjdhm_order}
  1872→array in sequence
  1873→. For each
  1874→{jjdt_coronet},
  1875→check `paces[coronet].tacks[0].state`
  1876→. If state is
  1877→{jjdpe_rough}
  1878→or
  1879→{jjdpe_bridled}:
  1880→.. Output `pace_coronet`,
  1881→`pace_silks` (from `tacks[0].silks`),
  1882→`pace_state`
  1883→.. Output `tack_text` from `tacks[0].text`
  1884→.. If state is
  1885→{jjdpe_bridled},
  1886→output `tack_direction` from `tacks[0].direction`
  1887→.. Stop walking (first match wins)
  1888→. If no actionable pace found, output only `heat_silks`, `paddock_file`, and `paddock_content`
  1889→
  1890→[[jjdo_parade]]
  1891→// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
  1892→==== {jjdo_parade}
  1893→
  1894→Display
  1895→{jjdhr_heat}
  1896→status in human-readable text format.
  1897→Output varies by format mode.
  1898→
  1899→{jjds_arguments}
  1900→
  1901→// ⟦axd_optional axd_defaulted⟧
  1902→* {jjda_file}
  1903→
  1904→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  1905→* {jjdt_firemark}
  1906→(positional) — target
  1907→{jjdhr_heat}
  1908→
  1909→// ⟦axd_optional axd_defaulted⟧
  1910→* `--format <mode>` — output format mode (default: `full`)
  1911→
  1912→// ⟦axd_optional⟧
  1913→* `--pace <coronet>` — target
  1914→{jjdt_coronet}
  1915→(required with `--format detail`)
  1916→
  1917→// ⟦axd_optional⟧
  1918→* `--remaining` — show only remaining paces (exclude
  1919→{jjdpe_complete}
  1920→and
  1921→{jjdpe_abandoned})
  1922→
  1923→*Format Modes*
  1924→
  1925→[cols="1,2,3"]
  1926→|===
  1927→| Mode | Purpose | Output
  1928→
  1929→| `overview`
  1930→| Quick status
  1931→| One line per pace: `[state] silks (₢coronet)`
  1932→
  1933→| `order`
  1934→| Dependency check
  1935→| Numbered: `N. [state] silks (₢coronet)`
  1936→
  1937→| `detail`
  1938→| Inspect one pace
  1939→| Full tack text for specified pace (requires `--pace`)
  1940→
  1941→| `full`
  1942→| Planning context
  1943→| Paddock content + all paces with tack text
  1944→|===
  1945→
  1946→{jjds_stdout} Human-readable text (not JSON).
  1947→
  1948→*Example output — overview:*
  1949→----
  1950→[abandoned] vvw-workbench-tabtarget (₢AAAAC)
  1951→[complete] install-arch-decision (₢AAAAI)
  1952→[rough] vvx-push-rename (₢AAAAJ)
  1953→----
  1954→
  1955→*Example output — order:*
  1956→----
  1957→1. [abandoned] vvw-workbench-tabtarget (₢AAAAC)
  1958→2. [complete] install-arch-decision (₢AAAAI)
  1959→3. [rough] vvx-push-rename (₢AAAAJ)
  1960→----
  1961→
  1962→*Example output — detail:*
  1963→----
  1964→Pace: vvx-push-rename (₢AAAAJ)
  1965→State: rough
  1966→Heat: ₣AA
  1967→
  1968→[full tack text...]
  1969→----
  1970→
  1971→{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
  1972→
  1973→{jjds_behavior}
  1974→
  1975→. Validate arguments (if `--format detail`, require `--pace`)
  1976→. Read
  1977→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1978→from file
  1979→. Verify
  1980→{jjdhr_heat}
  1981→exists with given
  1982→{jjdt_firemark}
  1983→. Match on format mode:
  1984→.. *overview*: For each pace in
  1985→{jjdhm_order},
  1986→print `[state] silks (coronet)` where silks is derived from `tacks[0].silks`
  1987→.. *order*: Same as overview with line numbers
  1988→.. *detail*: Find specified pace, print header and full tack text
  1989→.. *full*: Print heat header, paddock content, then each pace with tack text
  1990→
  1991→=== Steeplechase Operations
  1992→
  1993→The steeplechase is Job Jockey's session history, stored in git commits rather than the
  1994→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1995→JSON.
  1996→Commit messages follow patterns that
  1997→{jjdo_rein}
  1998→can parse to reconstruct session history.
  1999→
  2000→==== Commit Message Patterns
  2001→
  2002→All JJ commits use the format: `jjb:BRAND:IDENTITY[:ACTION]: message`
  2003→
  2004→* `jjb` — JJ brand prefix (literal)
  2005→* `BRAND` — repository identifier baked into arcanum at install time (e.g., "RBM")
  2006→* `IDENTITY` — ₢CORONET for pace-level, ₣FIREMARK for heat-level
  2007→* `ACTION` — single-letter code (required for all commits)
  2008→
  2009→*Standard notch* (created by {jjdo_notch}):
  2010→----
  2011→jjb:BRAND:₢XXXXX:n: commit message
  2012→
  2013→Co-Authored-By: ...
  2014→----
  2015→
  2016→Standard notch commits use `n` to denote routine progress tracking.
  2017→
  2018→*Pace-level chalk* (created by {jjdo_chalk}):
  2019→----
  2020→jjb:BRAND:₢XXXXX:A: description
  2021→
  2022→Co-Authored-By: ...
  2023→----
  2024→
  2025→Pace-level action codes:
  2026→
  2027→* `n` — Notch: standard commit
  2028→* `A` — APPROACH: proposed approach before work begins
  2029→* `W` — WRAP: pace completion summary
  2030→* `F` — FLY: autonomous execution began (bridled pace)
  2031→* `d` — discussion: significant decision (lowercase, can also be heat-level)
  2032→
  2033→*Heat-level operations*:
  2034→----
  2035→jjb:BRAND:₣XX:N: heat-silks
  2036→jjb:BRAND:₣XX:S: pace-silks
  2037→jjb:BRAND:₣XX:r: reordered
  2038→jjb:BRAND:₣XX:T: pace-silks
  2039→jjb:BRAND:₣XX:D: old-coronet → new-coronet
  2040→jjb:BRAND:₣XX:R: heat-silks
  2041→----
  2042→
  2043→Heat-level action codes:
  2044→
  2045→* `N` — Nominate: create new heat
  2046→* `S` — Slate: add new pace
  2047→* `r` — rail: reorder paces (lowercase)
  2048→* `T` — Tally: add tack to pace
  2049→* `D` — Draft: move pace between heats (uppercase, rare)
  2050→* `R` — Retire: archive heat (uppercase)
  2051→
  2052→[[jjdo_rein]]
  2053→// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
  2054→==== {jjdo_rein}
  2055→
  2056→Parse git history for steeplechase entries belonging to a
  2057→{jjdhr_heat}.
  2058→
  2059→{jjds_arguments}
  2060→
  2061→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  2062→* {jjdt_firemark}
  2063→(positional) — target
  2064→{jjdhr_heat}
  2065→
  2066→// ⟦axd_optional⟧
  2067→* `--limit` — maximum entries to return (default: 50)
  2068→
  2069→{jjds_stdout} JSON array of steeplechase entries:
  2070→[source,json]
  2071→----
  2072→[
  2073→  {
  2074→    "timestamp": "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM",
  2075→    "coronet": "₢XXXXX",
  2076→    "action": "n",
  2077→    "subject": "commit message"
  2078→  },
  2079→  {
  2080→    "timestamp": "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM",
  2081→    "coronet": "₢XXXXX",
  2082→    "action": "A",
  2083→    "subject": "description"
  2084→  },
  2085→  {
  2086→    "timestamp": "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM",
  2087→    "action": "N",
  2088→    "subject": "heat-silks"
  2089→  }
  2090→]
  2091→----
  2092→Field `coronet` is present for pace-level entries, absent for heat-level operations.
  2093→Field `action` is present for all commits, including standard notch commits (`n`).
  2094→Entries ordered newest first.
  2095→
  2096→{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
  2097→
  2098→{jjds_behavior}
  2099→
  2100→. Run `git log --all --extended-regexp --grep="^jjb:[^:]+:(₣XX|₢XX)" --format="%ai%x09%s"`
  2101→  (matches any brand; filtering is by identity, not brand)
  2102→. Parse each line: extract timestamp, identity, action (if present), and message
  2103→. For pace-level (₢CORONET): verify coronet belongs to target heat
  2104→. For heat-level (₣FIREMARK): verify firemark matches target heat
  2105→. Build JSON array, limit to requested count
  2106→. Output JSON
  2107→
  2108→[[jjdo_notch]]
  2109→// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
  2110→==== {jjdo_notch}
  2111→
  2112→Create a JJ-aware git commit with heat/pace context prefix.
  2113→
  2114→{jjds_arguments}
  2115→
  2116→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  2117→* {jjdt_coronet}
  2118→(positional) — active pace identity (embeds parent heat)
  2119→
  2120→// ⟦axd_optional⟧
  2121→* `--size-limit` — size limit in bytes for the commit guard (default: 50KB).
  2122→Use when the pace spec justifies legitimate large commits.
  2123→
  2124→{jjds_stdout} Commit hash
  2125→
  2126→{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
  2127→
  2128→{jjds_behavior}
  2129→
  2130→. Acquire lock (`refs/vvg/locks/vvx`)
  2131→. Stage changes (`git add -u`)
  2132→. Run size guard check (uses `--size-limit` if provided, otherwise 50KB default)
  2133→. Run `claude --print "Generate commit message for this diff: $(git diff --cached)"`
  2134→. Format full message: `jjb:BRAND:₢XXXXX:n: message\n\nCo-Authored-By: ...`
  2135→. Run `git commit -m "..."`
  2136→. Release lock
  2137→. Output commit hash
  2138→
  2139→[[jjdo_chalk]]
  2140→// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
  2141→==== {jjdo_chalk}
  2142→
  2143→Create an empty commit marking a steeplechase event.
  2144→
  2145→{jjds_arguments}
  2146→
  2147→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  2148→* identity (positional) — {jjdt_coronet} for pace-level, {jjdt_firemark} for heat-level discussion
  2149→
  2150→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  2151→* `--marker` — marker type: `A`, `W`, `F`, or `d` (single letter codes)
  2152→
  2153→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  2154→* `--description` — marker description text
  2155→
  2156→{jjds_stdout} Commit hash
  2157→
  2158→{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
  2159→
  2160→{jjds_behavior}
  2161→
  2162→. Acquire lock (`refs/vvg/locks/vvx`)
  2163→. Detect identity type by length (5 chars = coronet, 2 chars = firemark)
  2164→. Validate: `A`/`W`/`F` require coronet; `d` accepts either
  2165→. Format message:
  2166→.. If coronet: `jjb:BRAND:₢XXXXX:X: description\n\nCo-Authored-By: ...`
  2167→.. If firemark (discussion only): `jjb:BRAND:₣XX:d: description\n\nCo-Authored-By: ...`
  2168→. Run `git commit --allow-empty -m "..."`
  2169→. Release lock
  2170→. Output commit hash
  2171→

= Job Jockey Gallops Data Model
:doctype: book

// tag::mapping-section[]
//
// Category declarations:
// jjdt_:  Types (scalar value types with validation rules)

// jjdgm_: Gallops members

// jjdhe_: Heat enum values (status)
// jjdpr_: Pace record
// jjdpm_: Pace members
// jjdkr_: Tack record
// jjdkm_: Tack members
// jjdpe_: Tack state enum values
// jjdo_:  Operations
// jjds_:  Section headers (operation documentation structure)
// jjdz_:  Serialization concerns
//
// Variant patterns:
//   _s: plural form
//   _p: possessive form
//

// Types
:jjdt_coronet:    <<jjdt_coronet,Coronet>>

// Gallops record and members

// Heat record and members
:jjdhe_current:   <<jjdhe_current,current>>

:jjdhm_order:     <<jjdhm_order,order>>
:jjdhm_paddock:   <<jjdhm_paddock,paddock_file>>
:jjdhm_seed:      <<jjdhm_seed,next_pace_seed>>

// Pace record and members
:jjdpm_coronet:   <<jjdpm_coronet,coronet>>

// Pace/Tack state enum values (used in Tack)
:jjdpe_abandoned: <<jjdpe_abandoned,abandoned>>

// Tack record and members
:jjdkm_commit:    <<jjdkm_commit,commit>>

:jjdkm_silks:     <<jjdkm_silks,silks>>

// Arguments
:jjda_after:          <<jjda_after,--after>>

:jjda_pace:           <<jjda_pace,--pace>>

// CLI executor
:jjdx_vvx:   <<jjdx_vvx,vvx>>

// Operations
:jjdo_canter:         <<jjdo_canter,jjx_canter>>
:jjdo_chalk:          <<jjdo_chalk,jjx_chalk>>

:jjdo_muster:         <<jjdo_muster,jjx_muster>>

:jjdo_retire:         <<jjdo_retire,jjx_retire>>
:jjdo_saddle:         <<jjdo_saddle,jjx_saddle>>

:jjdo_tally:          <<jjdo_tally,jjx_tally>>

// Section headers
:jjds_arguments:        <<jjds_arguments,Arguments:>>

// Serialization
:jjdz_charset:  <<jjdz_charset,Charset>>
:jjdz_encoding: <<jjdz_encoding,Identity Encoding>>

// end::mapping-section[]

== Overview

The
{jjdgr_gallops}
is the persistent data store for Job Jockey.
It tracks
{jjdhr_heat_s}
(bounded initiatives) and their constituent
{jjdpr_pace_s}
(discrete actions).
Each
{jjdpr_pace}
maintains a history of
{jjdkr_tack_s}
(plan refinements).

== Design Principles

=== Exclusive JSON Ownership

All access to the
{jjdgr_gallops}
goes through {jjdx_cli}, the Job Jockey subcommand of the Voce Viva executor.
No jq or direct JSON manipulation ever touches this data.
This is an absolute architectural constraint, not a preference.

The rationale: writing reliable bash with jq pipelines requires fighting LLM training data.
The internet is full of bad bash.
Rust replaces fragile jq invocations with compiler-enforced correctness.
The compiler is tireless; humans are not.

=== Git as Journal

The
{jjdgr_gallops}
remains JSON specifically so git can serve as the journalling layer.
Every mutation creates a diffable commit.
Git owns the complete history; we need not implement our own audit trail.

The
{jjdkr_tack}
history within each
{jjdpr_pace}
exists for convenient postmortem analysis.
Git has the full history, but
{jjdkr_tack_s}
let us see plan evolution without spelunking commits.

=== Diff-Friendly Mutations

New
{jjdkr_tack_s}
prepend to position 0 rather than append.
This produces cleaner git diffs: prepending adds a line with trailing comma, while appending would modify the previous last line to add a comma.
Small discipline, large cumulative benefit.

The
{jjdhm_order}
array contains full
{jjdt_coronet_s}.
Each
{jjdt_coronet}
embeds its parent
{jjdt_firemark}
(redundant within a single
{jjdhr_heat}).
This redundancy is cheap and enables grep, copy-paste, and self-documenting JSON.

=== Crash-Safe Architecture

Bash handles locking via `git update-ref`.
{jjdx_cli} owns all
{jjdgr_gallops}
file access - reading, transforming, and writing.
{jjdx_cli} implements atomic writes internally (write to temp file, then rename).
If {jjdx_cli} crashes mid-transform, no partial writes occur.

The pattern:
[source,bash]
----
lock_acquire
vvx jjx gallops nominate --file gallops.json "my-heat" "260111" || { lock_release; die; }
lock_release
----

This keeps bash simple (just locking and invocation) while {jjdx_cli} owns all JSON file operations.

=== Deterministic Serialization

Implementations MUST serialize JSON object keys in deterministic order to minimize diff churn.
For `heats` and `paces` objects, use sorted key order (e.g., Rust `BTreeMap` instead of `HashMap`).
This ensures that reordering paces via
{jjdo_rail}
produces minimal diffs — only the `order` array changes, not the entire `paces` object.

=== Identity Encoding

{jjdt_firemark}
(`₣` prefix) and
{jjdt_coronet}
(`₢` prefix) are the canonical identities used in JSON keys, CLI arguments, and display.
The base64 encoding provides compact, URL-safe, shell-safe strings.
Internally, Rust may decode to integers for arithmetic, but the string form is authoritative.

=== Horse Racing Vocabulary

Job Jockey uses horse racing metaphors throughout.
This is intentional: vivid domain vocabulary helps humans hold complex systems in working memory.

* {jjdhr_heat} - a bounded initiative (like a race)
* {jjdpr_pace} - a discrete action (the rhythm of the race)
* {jjdkr_tack} - plan refinement (equipment; also "tacking" = adjusting course)
* {jjdt_firemark} - heat identity, `₣`-prefixed (hot-iron brand marking ownership)
* {jjdt_coronet} - pace identity, `₢`-prefixed (marking at the hoof line)
* {jjdt_silks} - display name (jockey's colors)
* {jjdgr_gallops} - the training grounds (where real work happens before race day)

== Types

[[jjdt_firemark]]
// ⟦axl_voices axt_string⟧
{jjdt_firemark}::
The identity of a
{jjdhr_heat}.
Format: `₣` + 2 base64 characters (e.g., `₣AB`).
The 2 base64 characters encode an integer 0-4095 (2^12).
Used as JSON keys and in CLI arguments.
Input may omit the `₣` prefix; output always includes it.

[[jjdt_coronet]]
// ⟦axl_voices axt_string⟧
{jjdt_coronet}::
The identity of a
{jjdpr_pace}.
Format: `₢` + 5 base64 characters (e.g., `₢ABCDE`).
Globally unique: the first 2 base64 characters are the parent
{jjdhr_heat_p}
encoded identity; the remaining 3 encode the pace index (0-262143, 2^18).
Used as JSON keys and in CLI arguments.
Input may omit the `₢` prefix; output always includes it.
+
*Reassignment:* When a
{jjdpr_pace}
is moved between
{jjdhr_heat_s}
via
{jjdo_draft},
the old
{jjdt_coronet}
becomes invalid and a new one is assigned using the destination
{jjdhr_heat_p}
{jjdhm_seed}.
The new
{jjdt_coronet}
embeds the destination
{jjdt_firemark}.
{jjdkr_tack}
history is preserved, with a note recording the original
{jjdt_coronet}.

[[jjdt_silks]]
// ⟦axl_voices axt_string⟧
{jjdt_silks}::
A kebab-case display name.
Used for human-readable identification of
{jjdhr_heat_s}
and
{jjdpr_pace_s}.
Pattern: `[a-z0-9]+(-[a-z0-9]+)*`

== Records

=== {jjdgr_gallops}

[[jjdgr_gallops]]
// ⟦axl_voices axr_record_json⟧
{jjdgr_gallops}::
The root JSON structure containing all Job Jockey data.
Singleton record persisted at `.claude/jjm/jjg_gallops.json`.

==== Members

* [[jjdgm_seed]]
// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
{jjdgm_seed}
is the next
{jjdt_firemark}
to allocate, stored as 2 base64 characters.
Incremented by
{jjdo_nominate}.

=== {jjdhr_heat}

[[jjdhr_heat]]
// ⟦axl_voices axr_record_json⟧
{jjdhr_heat}::
A bounded initiative with coherent goals.
Contains zero or more
{jjdpr_pace_s}
representing discrete actions.
Keyed in JSON by
{jjdt_firemark}.

==== Members

* [[jjdhm_firemark]]
// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
{jjdhm_firemark}
is the
{jjdhr_heat_p}
primary key.
Type:
{jjdt_firemark}.
Implicit from JSON key; not stored as separate field.

* [[jjdhm_silks]]
// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
{jjdhm_silks}
is the
{jjdhr_heat_p}
display name.
Type:
{jjdt_silks}.

* [[jjdhm_created]]
// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
{jjdhm_created}
records when the
{jjdhr_heat}
was nominated.
Format: YYMMDD.

* [[jjdhm_status]]
// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
{jjdhm_status}
indicates the
{jjdhr_heat_p}
lifecycle state.
Values:
{jjdhe_current}
or
{jjdhe_retired}.

* [[jjdhm_order]]
// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required axd_repeated⟧
{jjdhm_order}
lists
{jjdt_coronet_s}
in execution sequence.
May be reordered via
{jjdo_rail}.

* [[jjdhm_seed]]
// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
{jjdhm_seed}
is the next
{jjdt_coronet}
index to allocate, stored as 3 base64 characters.
Incremented by
{jjdo_slate}.

* [[jjdhm_paddock]]
// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
{jjdhm_paddock}
is the path to the paddock file containing
{jjdhr_heat}
context and background.
Format: `.claude/jjm/jjp_XX.md` where XX is the base64-encoded
{jjdt_firemark}
(without `₣` prefix).
Created by
{jjdo_nominate}.

==== Status Values

[[jjdhe_current]]
// ⟦axl_voices axt_enum_value⟧
{jjdhe_current}::
The
{jjdhr_heat}
is active and accepting work.

[[jjdhe_retired]]
// ⟦axl_voices axt_enum_value⟧
{jjdhe_retired}::
The
{jjdhr_heat}
is complete and archived.

=== {jjdpr_pace}

== Serialization

[[jjdz_charset]]
{jjdz_charset}::
The 64-character alphabet for identity encoding.
Uses URL-safe base64 (RFC 4648 §5):
`ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789-_`
Position in charset determines numeric value (A=0, B=1, ..., `-`=62, `_`=63).
This alphabet is safe for JSON keys, URLs, shell, git refs, and filenames.
Matches Rust `base64::engine::general_purpose::URL_SAFE_NO_PAD`.

[[jjdz_encoding]]
{jjdz_encoding}::
The algorithm for encoding
{jjdt_firemark}
and
{jjdt_coronet}
values.
+
*{jjdt_firemark} (Heat identity):*
+
. Format: `₣` + 2 base64 characters
. Given integer H (0-4095): encode as `₣` + `charset[H/64]` + `charset[H%64]`
. To decode `₣XY`: H = pos(X) * 64 + pos(Y)
+
*{jjdt_coronet} (Pace identity):*
+
. Format: `₢` + 5 base64 characters
. First 2 characters encode the parent Heat (same as {jjdt_firemark} without prefix)
. Last 3 characters encode the pace index P (0-262143)
. Given Heat H and pace index P: encode as `₢` + `charset[H/64]` + `charset[H%64]` + `charset[P/4096]` + `charset[(P/64)%64]` + `charset[P%64]`
. To decode `₢XYZAB`: H from XY, P = pos(Z) * 4096 + pos(A) * 64 + pos(B)
+
*Input flexibility:*
+
CLI commands accept identities with or without prefix.
Length determines type: 2 base64 chars = {jjdt_firemark}, 5 base64 chars = {jjdt_coronet}.
Output always includes the prefix (`₣` or `₢`).

=== Legacy Format Acceptance

During migration period, implementations MUST accept legacy format on read:

* {jjdpr_pace} with `silks` field → copy to all {jjdkr_tack_s} missing {jjdkm_silks}, discard from {jjdpr_pace}
* {jjdkr_tack} without {jjdkm_silks} → inherit from {jjdpr_pace} (error if {jjdpr_pace} also missing silks)
* {jjdkr_tack} without {jjdkm_commit} → use `0000000`

Implementations MUST write only canonical format:

* {jjdpr_pace} without `silks` field
* {jjdkr_tack} with {jjdkm_silks} (required)
* {jjdkr_tack} with {jjdkm_commit} (required, `0000000` if unknown)

== Commit Message Architecture

All JJ commits include an action code that serves multiple purposes:
- **Parsing steeplechase history**: Distinguishes between different operation types
- **Semantic clarity**: Indicates the nature and context of each commit
- **Consistency**: Applies uniformly across pace-level and heat-level operations

== Operations

Operations are implemented as {jjdx_cli} subcommands.
{jjdx_cli} owns all
{jjdgr_gallops}
file access (read, transform, atomic write).
Bash handles locking via `git update-ref` and invokes {jjdx_cli} with
{jjda_file}.

=== Steeplechase Operations

The steeplechase is Job Jockey's session history, stored in git commits rather than the
{jjdgr_gallops}
JSON.
Commit messages follow patterns that
{jjdo_rein}
can parse to reconstruct session history.

==== Commit Message Patterns

All JJ commits use the format: `jjb:BRAND:IDENTITY[:ACTION]: message`

* `jjb` — JJ brand prefix (literal)
* `BRAND` — repository identifier baked into arcanum at install time (e.g., "RBM")
* `IDENTITY` — ₢CORONET for pace-level, ₣FIREMARK for heat-level
* `ACTION` — single-letter code (required for all commits)

*Standard notch* (created by {jjdo_notch}):
----
jjb:BRAND:₢XXXXX:n: commit message

Co-Authored-By: ...
----

Standard notch commits use `n` to denote routine progress tracking.

*Pace-level chalk* (created by {jjdo_chalk}):
----
jjb:BRAND:₢XXXXX:A: description

Co-Authored-By: ...
----

Pace-level action codes:

* `n` — Notch: standard commit
* `A` — APPROACH: proposed approach before work begins
* `W` — WRAP: pace completion summary
* `F` — FLY: autonomous execution began (bridled pace)
* `d` — discussion: significant decision (lowercase, can also be heat-level)

*Heat-level operations*:
----
jjb:BRAND:₣XX:N: heat-silks
jjb:BRAND:₣XX:S: pace-silks
jjb:BRAND:₣XX:r: reordered
jjb:BRAND:₣XX:T: pace-silks
jjb:BRAND:₣XX:D: old-coronet → new-coronet
jjb:BRAND:₣XX:R: heat-silks
----

Heat-level action codes:

* `N` — Nominate: create new heat
* `S` — Slate: add new pace
* `r` — rail: reorder paces (lowercase)
* `T` — Tally: add tack to pace
* `D` — Draft: move pace between heats (uppercase, rare)
* `R` — Retire: archive heat (uppercase)

// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
==== {jjdo_rein}

Parse git history for steeplechase entries belonging to a
{jjdhr_heat}.

{jjds_arguments}

// ⟦axd_required⟧
* {jjdt_firemark}
(positional) — target
{jjdhr_heat}

// ⟦axd_optional⟧
* `--limit` — maximum entries to return (default: 50)

{jjds_stdout} JSON array of steeplechase entries:
[source,json]
----
[
  {
    "timestamp": "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM",
    "coronet": "₢XXXXX",
    "action": "n",
    "subject": "commit message"
  },
  {
    "timestamp": "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM",
    "coronet": "₢XXXXX",
    "action": "A",
    "subject": "description"
  },
  {
    "timestamp": "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM",
    "action": "N",
    "subject": "heat-silks"
  }
]
----
Field `coronet` is present for pace-level entries, absent for heat-level operations.
Field `action` is present for all commits, including standard notch commits (`n`).
Entries ordered newest first.

{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.

{jjds_behavior}

. Run `git log --all --extended-regexp --grep="^jjb:[^:]+:(₣XX|₢XX)" --format="%ai%x09%s"`
  (matches any brand; filtering is by identity, not brand)
. Parse each line: extract timestamp, identity, action (if present), and message
. For pace-level (₢CORONET): verify coronet belongs to target heat
. For heat-level (₣FIREMARK): verify firemark matches target heat
. Build JSON array, limit to requested count
. Output JSON

== Overview

The
{jjdgr_gallops}
is the persistent data store for Job Jockey.
It tracks
{jjdhr_heat_s}
(bounded initiatives) and their constituent
{jjdpr_pace_s}
(discrete actions).
Each
{jjdpr_pace}
maintains a history of
{jjdkr_tack_s}
(plan refinements).

== Design Principles

=== Exclusive JSON Ownership

All access to the
{jjdgr_gallops}
goes through {jjdx_cli}, the Job Jockey subcommand of the Voce Viva executor.
No jq or direct JSON manipulation ever touches this data.
This is an absolute architectural constraint, not a preference.

The rationale: writing reliable bash with jq pipelines requires fighting LLM training data.
The internet is full of bad bash.
Rust replaces fragile jq invocations with compiler-enforced correctness.
The compiler is tireless; humans are not.

=== Git as Journal

The
{jjdgr_gallops}
remains JSON specifically so git can serve as the journalling layer.
Every mutation creates a diffable commit.
Git owns the complete history; we need not implement our own audit trail.

The
{jjdkr_tack}
history within each
{jjdpr_pace}
exists for convenient postmortem analysis.
Git has the full history, but
{jjdkr_tack_s}
let us see plan evolution without spelunking commits.

=== Diff-Friendly Mutations

New
{jjdkr_tack_s}
prepend to position 0 rather than append.
This produces cleaner git diffs: prepending adds a line with trailing comma, while appending would modify the previous last line to add a comma.
Small discipline, large cumulative benefit.

The
{jjdhm_order}
array contains full
{jjdt_coronet_s}.
Each
{jjdt_coronet}
embeds its parent
{jjdt_firemark}
(redundant within a single
{jjdhr_heat}).
This redundancy is cheap and enables grep, copy-paste, and self-documenting JSON.

=== Crash-Safe Architecture

Bash handles locking via `git update-ref`.
{jjdx_cli} owns all
{jjdgr_gallops}
file access - reading, transforming, and writing.
{jjdx_cli} implements atomic writes internally (write to temp file, then rename).
If {jjdx_cli} crashes mid-transform, no partial writes occur.

The pattern:
[source,bash]
----
lock_acquire
vvx jjx gallops nominate --file gallops.json "my-heat" "260111" || { lock_release; die; }
lock_release
----

This keeps bash simple (just locking and invocation) while {jjdx_cli} owns all JSON file operations.

=== Deterministic Serialization

Implementations MUST serialize JSON object keys in deterministic order to minimize diff churn.
For `heats` and `paces` objects, use sorted key order (e.g., Rust `BTreeMap` instead of `HashMap`).
This ensures that reordering paces via
{jjdo_rail}
produces minimal diffs — only the `order` array changes, not the entire `paces` object.

=== Identity Encoding

{jjdt_firemark}
(`₣` prefix) and
{jjdt_coronet}
(`₢` prefix) are the canonical identities used in JSON keys, CLI arguments, and display.
The base64 encoding provides compact, URL-safe, shell-safe strings.
Internally, Rust may decode to integers for arithmetic, but the string form is authoritative.

=== Horse Racing Vocabulary

Job Jockey uses horse racing metaphors throughout.
This is intentional: vivid domain vocabulary helps humans hold complex systems in working memory.

* {jjdhr_heat} - a bounded initiative (like a race)
* {jjdpr_pace} - a discrete action (the rhythm of the race)
* {jjdkr_tack} - plan refinement (equipment; also "tacking" = adjusting course)
* {jjdt_firemark} - heat identity, `₣`-prefixed (hot-iron brand marking ownership)
* {jjdt_coronet} - pace identity, `₢`-prefixed (marking at the hoof line)
* {jjdt_silks} - display name (jockey's colors)
* {jjdgr_gallops} - the training grounds (where real work happens before race day)

== Serialization

[[jjdz_charset]]
{jjdz_charset}::
The 64-character alphabet for identity encoding.
Uses URL-safe base64 (RFC 4648 §5):
`ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789-_`
Position in charset determines numeric value (A=0, B=1, ..., `-`=62, `_`=63).
This alphabet is safe for JSON keys, URLs, shell, git refs, and filenames.
Matches Rust `base64::engine::general_purpose::URL_SAFE_NO_PAD`.

[[jjdz_encoding]]
{jjdz_encoding}::
The algorithm for encoding
{jjdt_firemark}
and
{jjdt_coronet}
values.
+
*{jjdt_firemark} (Heat identity):*
+
. Format: `₣` + 2 base64 characters
. Given integer H (0-4095): encode as `₣` + `charset[H/64]` + `charset[H%64]`
. To decode `₣XY`: H = pos(X) * 64 + pos(Y)
+
*{jjdt_coronet} (Pace identity):*
+
. Format: `₢` + 5 base64 characters
. First 2 characters encode the parent Heat (same as {jjdt_firemark} without prefix)
. Last 3 characters encode the pace index P (0-262143)
. Given Heat H and pace index P: encode as `₢` + `charset[H/64]` + `charset[H%64]` + `charset[P/4096]` + `charset[(P/64)%64]` + `charset[P%64]`
. To decode `₢XYZAB`: H from XY, P = pos(Z) * 4096 + pos(A) * 64 + pos(B)
+
*Input flexibility:*
+
CLI commands accept identities with or without prefix.
Length determines type: 2 base64 chars = {jjdt_firemark}, 5 base64 chars = {jjdt_coronet}.
Output always includes the prefix (`₣` or `₢`).

=== Legacy Format Acceptance

During migration period, implementations MUST accept legacy format on read:

* {jjdpr_pace} with `silks` field → copy to all {jjdkr_tack_s} missing {jjdkm_silks}, discard from {jjdpr_pace}
* {jjdkr_tack} without {jjdkm_silks} → inherit from {jjdpr_pace} (error if {jjdpr_pace} also missing silks)
* {jjdkr_tack} without {jjdkm_commit} → use `0000000`

Implementations MUST write only canonical format:

* {jjdpr_pace} without `silks` field
* {jjdkr_tack} with {jjdkm_silks} (required)
* {jjdkr_tack} with {jjdkm_commit} (required, `0000000` if unknown)

== Commit Message Architecture

All JJ commits include an action code that serves multiple purposes:
- **Parsing steeplechase history**: Distinguishes between different operation types
- **Semantic clarity**: Indicates the nature and context of each commit
- **Consistency**: Applies uniformly across pace-level and heat-level operations

=== Steeplechase Operations

The steeplechase is Job Jockey's session history, stored in git commits rather than the
{jjdgr_gallops}
JSON.
Commit messages follow patterns that
{jjdo_rein}
can parse to reconstruct session history.

==== Commit Message Patterns

All JJ commits use the format: `jjb:BRAND:IDENTITY[:ACTION]: message`

* `jjb` — JJ brand prefix (literal)
* `BRAND` — repository identifier baked into arcanum at install time (e.g., "RBM")
* `IDENTITY` — ₢CORONET for pace-level, ₣FIREMARK for heat-level
* `ACTION` — single-letter code (required for all commits)

*Standard notch* (created by {jjdo_notch}):
----
jjb:BRAND:₢XXXXX:n: commit message

Co-Authored-By: ...
----

Standard notch commits use `n` to denote routine progress tracking.

*Pace-level chalk* (created by {jjdo_chalk}):
----
jjb:BRAND:₢XXXXX:A: description

Co-Authored-By: ...
----

Pace-level action codes:

* `n` — Notch: standard commit
* `A` — APPROACH: proposed approach before work begins
* `W` — WRAP: pace completion summary
* `F` — FLY: autonomous execution began (bridled pace)
* `d` — discussion: significant decision (lowercase, can also be heat-level)

*Heat-level operations*:
----
jjb:BRAND:₣XX:N: heat-silks
jjb:BRAND:₣XX:S: pace-silks
jjb:BRAND:₣XX:r: reordered
jjb:BRAND:₣XX:T: pace-silks
jjb:BRAND:₣XX:D: old-coronet → new-coronet
jjb:BRAND:₣XX:R: heat-silks
----

Heat-level action codes:

* `N` — Nominate: create new heat
* `S` — Slate: add new pace
* `r` — rail: reorder paces (lowercase)
* `T` — Tally: add tack to pace
* `D` — Draft: move pace between heats (uppercase, rare)
* `R` — Retire: archive heat (uppercase)

[[jjdo_rein]]
// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
==== {jjdo_rein}

Parse git history for steeplechase entries belonging to a
{jjdhr_heat}.

{jjds_arguments}

// ⟦axd_required⟧
* {jjdt_firemark}
(positional) — target
{jjdhr_heat}

// ⟦axd_optional⟧
* `--limit` — maximum entries to return (default: 50)

{jjds_stdout} JSON array of steeplechase entries:
[source,json]
----
[
  {
    "timestamp": "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM",
    "coronet": "₢XXXXX",
    "action": "n",
    "subject": "commit message"
  },
  {
    "timestamp": "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM",
    "coronet": "₢XXXXX",
    "action": "A",
    "subject": "description"
  },
  {
    "timestamp": "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM",
    "action": "N",
    "subject": "heat-silks"
  }
]
----
Field `coronet` is present for pace-level entries, absent for heat-level operations.
Field `action` is present for all commits, including standard notch commits (`n`).
Entries ordered newest first.

{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.

{jjds_behavior}

. Run `git log --all --extended-regexp --grep="^jjb:[^:]+:(₣XX|₢XX)" --format="%ai%x09%s"`
  (matches any brand; filtering is by identity, not brand)
. Parse each line: extract timestamp, identity, action (if present), and message
. For pace-level (₢CORONET): verify coronet belongs to target heat
. For heat-level (₣FIREMARK): verify firemark matches target heat
. Build JSON array, limit to requested count
. Output JSON

[[jjdo_notch]]
// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
==== {jjdo_notch}

Create a JJ-aware git commit with heat/pace context prefix.

{jjds_arguments}

// ⟦axd_required⟧
* {jjdt_coronet}
(positional) — active pace identity (embeds parent heat)

// ⟦axd_optional⟧
* `--size-limit` — size limit in bytes for the commit guard (default: 50KB).
Use when the pace spec justifies legitimate large commits.

{jjds_stdout} Commit hash

{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.

{jjds_behavior}

. Acquire lock (`refs/vvg/locks/vvx`)
. Stage changes (`git add -u`)
. Run size guard check (uses `--size-limit` if provided, otherwise 50KB default)
. Run `claude --print "Generate commit message for this diff: $(git diff --cached)"`
. Format full message: `jjb:BRAND:₢XXXXX:n: message\n\nCo-Authored-By: ...`
. Run `git commit -m "..."`
. Release lock
. Output commit hash

[[jjdo_chalk]]
// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
==== {jjdo_chalk}

Create an empty commit marking a steeplechase event.

{jjds_arguments}

// ⟦axd_required⟧
* identity (positional) — {jjdt_coronet} for pace-level, {jjdt_firemark} for heat-level discussion

// ⟦axd_required⟧
* `--marker` — marker type: `A`, `W`, `F`, or `d` (single letter codes)

// ⟦axd_required⟧
* `--description` — marker description text

{jjds_stdout} Commit hash

{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.

{jjds_behavior}

. Acquire lock (`refs/vvg/locks/vvx`)
. Detect identity type by length (5 chars = coronet, 2 chars = firemark)
. Validate: `A`/`W`/`F` require coronet; `d` accepts either
. Format message:
.. If coronet: `jjb:BRAND:₢XXXXX:X: description\n\nCo-Authored-By: ...`
.. If firemark (discussion only): `jjb:BRAND:₣XX:d: description\n\nCo-Authored-By: ...`
. Run `git commit --allow-empty -m "..."`
. Release lock
. Output commit hash
